text.title: PM Tools Comparison Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the PM Tools Comparison methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Weights MUST be (Core 30%, Usability 25%, Integrations 20%, Enterprise 15%, Cost 10%) — changes require an explicit ADR.
rule.rationale: weight drift makes comparisons non-comparable across reports.

rule.statement: Each per-criterion score MUST cite >=1 evidence anchor (PoC observation, vendor docs, customer reference); scores without evidence are rejected.
rule.rationale: vibes scoring biases toward the loudest stakeholder.

rule.statement: PoC duration MUST be >=10 working days per tool; shorter PoCs miss workflow friction that surfaces only in week 2.
rule.rationale: 1-week PoCs catch the demo flow but miss the operational reality.

rule.statement: TCO MUST be computed over 3 years including licence + migration + training; single-year cost is rejected.
rule.rationale: single-year cost hides migration spike and renewal escalation.

rule.statement: ADR MUST name a single decision_owner AND capture dissenting opinions in an addendum; consensus-only ADRs hide future re-litigation.
rule.rationale: captured dissents prevent the same debate from re-opening in 6 months.

rule.statement: Recommendation MUST be the tool with the highest weighted total OR carry an explicit override rationale.

rationale.source: Anti-bias practice (Kahneman, Thinking Fast and Slow)

rule.rationale: recommendations that don't match the scores erode trust in the process.
